skill.name: baoyu-format-markdown
skill.description: Formats plain text or Markdown into structured Markdown with frontmatter, summaries, headings, lists, emphasis, code blocks, and CJK typography cleanup.
skill.summary: Turn drafts into structured Markdown with frontmatter and typography cleanup.
skill.author: jimliu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command-like patterns, but most SKILL.md hits are Markdown documentation examples or formatting syntax. One confirmed issue remains: scripts/autocorrect.ts builds a shell command with a user-controlled file path, creating command injection risk. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, environment access, or malicious intent was found.

high_findings.title: Shell Command Injection Through File Path
high_findings.description: scripts/autocorrect.ts imports child_process and passes a template string to execSync. The file path comes from the CLI argument flow in scripts/main.ts, so a crafted path containing quotes or shell metacharacters can break out of the quoted argument and run arbitrary shell commands.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interpolates a user-supplied file path into an execSync shell command. Quoting the path does not prevent injection when the value can contain quote characters.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs users to run npx with bun and the script invokes npx autocorrect-node. This executes external tooling without a pinned package version in the documented command path, creating supply-chain and reproducibility risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external execution path is explicit and repeatable. The finding is not evidence of malware, but unpinned npx execution is a real marketplace risk.

medium_findings.title: In-Place File Modification
medium_findings.description: scripts/main.ts reads and writes the target file directly, and the skill also offers a typography-only mode that modifies the original file. This is expected formatter behavior, but it can overwrite user content if the wrong path is supplied or backup handling is bypassed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is clear in the implementation and documentation. It is legitimate for a formatter, but it has user-data impact.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Text
low_findings.description: Most SKILL.md external command findings are Markdown examples, inline code, tables, and fenced commands that document formatter usage. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts did not correspond to cryptographic operations or system probing in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows documentation content and Markdown syntax, not executable code for cryptography or reconnaissance. The safe interpretation is supported by the surrounding workflow text.

low_findings.title: Benign Unicode Escape Use
low_findings.description: scripts/quotes.ts uses Unicode escapes for typographic quotation marks. This is not obfuscation and matches the documented quote replacement feature.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The escape sequences are immediately used as replacement quote characters. There is no hidden control flow, decoding layer, or suspicious payload.

low_findings.title: Hidden Configuration File Access
low_findings.description: The skill checks project and home EXTEND.md paths under .baoyu-skills for optional preferences. This is a bounded configuration lookup, but users should know it may read formatter preferences from hidden directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented paths are narrow and preference-oriented, so this is not secret harvesting. It is still a hidden-file access pattern worth disclosing.

dangerous_patterns.title: execSync With Interpolated Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The autocorrect wrapper constructs a shell command using template interpolation instead of spawn or execFile with an argument array.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is present on a single reviewed line and directly uses execSync. This is the core cause of the command injection finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Write To User-Supplied Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The formatter writes formatted content back to the provided file path before running spacing fixes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicit and expected for the tool. It is dangerous only when the path is unintended or the caller expected a copy-only operation.

content.value_statement: Unstructured drafts are hard to scan and publish consistently. This skill converts plain text or rough Markdown into organized Markdown with frontmatter and typography cleanup.
actual_capabilities: Detects plain text versus existing Markdown before choosing a formatting path.
Creates or normalizes YAML frontmatter with title, slug, summary, and cover image fields.
Adds readable Markdown structure such as headings, bold emphasis, lists, blockquotes, and code fences.
Creates formatted output files using the original filename plus a formatted suffix.
Runs a typography cleanup script for CJK emphasis, mixed-language spacing, and optional quote replacement.
Supports optional project or user preferences through EXTEND.md files.
limitations: The helper script can overwrite files when typography-only mode is selected.
The autocorrect command currently uses shell interpolation and should be reviewed before use with untrusted paths.
It does not verify facts, improve arguments, or rewrite the source viewpoint.
Generated titles and summaries may need human review before publication.

use_cases.title: Prepare Blog Drafts
use_cases.description: Turn a plain article draft into publishable Markdown with frontmatter, headings, lists, and summary metadata.

use_cases.title: Clean Bilingual Notes
use_cases.description: Apply CJK and English spacing cleanup after organizing notes into readable Markdown sections.

use_cases.title: Standardize Repository Content
use_cases.description: Format project documentation drafts into consistent Markdown files before review or publication.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single plain text file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this draft as Markdown and save the result as a formatted copy: path/to/draft.txt

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Existing Markdown
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve structure while preserving the original meaning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Optimize this Markdown article with frontmatter, cleaner headings, lists, and typography fixes: path/to/article.md

prompt_templates.title: Keep Structure, Fix Typography
prompt_templates.scenario: Use minimal changes for an already edited article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the existing Markdown structure and only apply typography cleanup to a formatted copy: path/to/post.md

prompt_templates.title: Apply Publishing Preferences
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow using project or user EXTEND.md preferences.
prompt_templates.prompt: Format this article using the available baoyu-format-markdown preferences, then summarize the changes made: path/to/final.md

output_examples.input: A plain text article with several points in one paragraph and no metadata.
output_examples.output: A formatted Markdown article with frontmatter, a concise title, a short summary, separated paragraphs, and a bullet list.

output_examples.input: An existing Markdown post with headings and mixed Chinese and English text.
output_examples.output: A cleaned Markdown post with preserved structure, normalized frontmatter, improved CJK spacing, and corrected emphasis punctuation.

output_examples.input: A documentation draft that mentions commands and examples inline.
output_examples.output: A structured document with command names formatted inline, examples placed in fenced blocks, and key points highlighted.

best_practices: Use the formatted-copy workflow for important files before applying typography-only edits.
Review generated titles, slugs, and summaries before publishing.
Use simple file paths without shell metacharacters until the command execution issue is fixed.
anti_patterns: Do not run the helper script on file paths from untrusted repositories without review.
Do not use the skill to rewrite opinions or add facts that are not in the source.
Do not rely on generated metadata as final editorial approval.

faq.question: Does this skill change the meaning of my writing?
faq.answer: It is designed to preserve the original meaning and add structure only. You should still review the final file.

faq.question: Can it work with existing Markdown?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects existing Markdown and can optimize it, preserve the structure, or apply typography fixes only.

faq.question: Does it create frontmatter?
faq.answer: Yes. It can add or normalize YAML frontmatter with title, slug, summary, and cover image fields.

faq.question: Does it support Chinese and English mixed text?
faq.answer: Yes. The formatter includes CJK-friendly emphasis handling and optional mixed-language spacing cleanup.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on any file path?
faq.answer: No. The current autocorrect wrapper has command injection risk with crafted paths, so trusted simple paths are recommended.

faq.question: Does it fetch data from the internet?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests was found in the skill code. The documented npx commands may install external tooling.
